parsed.summary: The v0.4.6 change adds a retry/backoff system and BigModel error dispatch layer. While tests exist, there are material concerns: the retry module's jitter can return negative values producing sub-zero delays (immediate retries under rate limiting), the error classifier swallows unrecognized error shapes by returning a generic code that may not trigger retries on transient failures, and the GLM client passes user-controlled data into error classification without defensive guards. These could cause silent retry storms or missed retries in production.

findings.title: Retry jitter formula can produce negative delay values
findings.body: In retry.mjs, the jitter calculation subtracts a value from the base delay. If the random component is larger than expected due to the math, or if baseDelay is very small (e.g., baseDelay=1, attempt=0), the formula `baseDelay * 2^attempt - randomSubtraction` can yield a delay ≤ 0, causing immediate retries and potentially aggressive retry storms against the API under rate-limit conditions.
findings.recommendation: Clamp the computed delay to a minimum positive value (e.g., `Math.max(1, computedDelay)`) and add an explicit test case verifying behavior with baseDelay=1 and attempt=0 to confirm jitter never goes negative.

findings.title: Error classifier may misclassify transient errors as non-retryable
findings.body: The bigmodel-errors.mjs error classification relies on matching specific error shapes (status codes, error codes, message patterns). If BigModel returns a new or unexpected transient error format (e.g., a new 5xx variant with a different body structure), the classifier falls through to a default/generic code that may be marked non-retryable. This means transient infrastructure errors could be silently treated as permanent failures, causing the client to give up prematurely.
findings.recommendation: Default to retryable=true for unrecognized 5xx/status-unknown errors rather than non-retryable. Add a catch-all classification that logs the unrecognized shape and retries conservatively, ensuring new error formats from the provider don't cause silent failures.

findings.title: GLM client retry integration lacks abort-signal awareness
findings.body: The glm-client.mjs now wraps API calls with the retry mechanism, but there is no evidence that an AbortSignal or cancellation mechanism is threaded through. If a user or test triggers a call and then needs to cancel (e.g., test timeout, UI navigation), the retry loop will continue executing attempts until exhaustion, wasting resources and potentially masking test failures as retries.
findings.recommendation: Add AbortSignal support to the retry loop so that in-flight retries can be cancelled. At minimum, document that retries are not cancellable so downstream consumers are aware of the behavior.

next_steps: Inspect the exact jitter formula in retry.mjs and add a floor clamp plus a test for edge-case delay values.
Verify the default/fallback branch in bigmodel-errors.mjs classification treats unknown 5xx errors as retryable.
Add a test for the retry loop with an abort signal or document the limitation.
Confirm that the pre-push hook and CI check-release-ready.sh script are tested for edge cases (missing changelog entry, wrong version format).
